original_text:
### Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
- Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
- Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.
- Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.
- Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).
- Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
- All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

relations.note: The dark-section adjacency check is enabled by declared section backgrounds.
Assembly-time symbol assignment relies on declared footnote-symbol consumption.
Both rules govern safe direct stacking of modular sections around core-inserted components.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Ensure modular sections stack reliably across email clients.
rules.rule_text: Each email section must be a self-contained table-based block using the 600px email canvas and 32px top/bottom section padding. Each section declares its background color, has no external margins, and is assembled by direct stacking.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Provide the assembler the declared properties needed to compose sections safely.
rules.rule_text: Section metadata must declare the background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), the footnote symbols consumed, and whether the section contains a CTA.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time**

Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Prevent footnote-symbol collisions as independently authored sections are assembled.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols must be superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time. When more than one referenced claim exists, sections use placeholder tokens such as `{{fn1}}` rather than hard-coded daggers.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Keep independently inserted locked components visually and structurally independent.
rules.rule_text: Locked header, footer, and ISI components are inserted by the core team. Sections adjacent to those locked components must not include decorative elements that visually depend on the section above or below, such as a wave intended to continue into the footer.

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections
Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain visual separation and readable pacing between dark sections.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark-background sections; the assembler enforces this from declared section backgrounds.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Preserve approved rendering across constrained email clients, including Outlook.
rules.rule_text: Email code must express colors as HEX and sizing in px, use inline CSS only for email-client compatibility, and duplicate background colors in `bgcolor` attributes for Outlook.
